timeline_html:
Summary. Jena first noticed her shedding more than a year ago, and it has tended to come and go in waves since then. She remains on hormonal treatment that began back in April 2022.

Your hair-loss triggers.

  • Started or stopped birth control / hormonal treatment (ongoing; started Apr 2022)

Timeline forecast. Based on the usual 2–4‑month lag after a hormonal change, her heaviest shedding likely appeared around mid‑2022 and gradually eased through late 2022 to early 2023. Because she continues on hormonal therapy, fluctuations can still happen, but current shedding should keep settling. Baby hairs usually show within 1–2 months after stabilization, which fits her recent regrowth. Dominant driver: ongoing hormonal treatment since Apr 2022, matching the long‑term pattern and timing of her waves.

Where you are now. You appear to be in the early regrowth phase, as new baby hairs have started to emerge and shedding is less extreme. That’s a good sign that your follicles are re‑entering healthier cycles—keep steady with balanced nutrition and stress care. 🌱
